claim: peter-evans/create-pull-request is a GitHub Action that detects workspace changes, commits them to a branch, and creates or updates a PR; current version is v8.1.1 (Apr 2026); requires contents: write and pull-requests: write.
quote: automates pull request creation by detecting repository changes in the Actions workspace—including new files, modified files, and commits—then commits them to a branch and creates or updates a pull request

claim: PRs created with the default GITHUB_TOKEN cannot trigger downstream workflows; the standard workarounds are a PAT, a GitHub App token, or a machine-account fork.
quote: Pull requests created with the default token "cannot trigger other workflows" ... GitHub App tokens *do* trigger both push and pull request workflows, enabling downstream automation that GITHUB_TOKEN cannot accomplish.

claim: repository_dispatch carries a custom event_type plus client_payload (max 10 top-level properties, 65,535 chars) and only runs on the default branch; workflow_dispatch accepts up to 25 typed inputs and can target an arbitrary ref.
quote: repository_dispatch ... Custom event_type ... client_payload with up to 10 top-level properties and maximum 65,535 characters total ... Runs on the default branch where the workflow file exists.

claim: Since Sep 2022, GITHUB_TOKEN can dispatch workflow_dispatch and repository_dispatch events directly — these are explicit calls so they do not chain into recursive runs.
quote: Customers can use the GITHUB_TOKEN with both workflow_dispatch and repository_dispatch events to trigger workflows, as these are explicit calls made by the customer and not likely to end up in a loop.

claim: A community discussion documenting the recurring confusion that PRs created by GITHUB_TOKEN do not fire pull_request workflow runs.
quote: Pull request created in a GitHub action does not trigger workflows with pull_request trigger

claim: anthropics/claude-code-action v1 lets a workflow run Claude Code on GitHub-hosted runners and create a PR; it auto-detects mode based on triggers (issue/PR comments, issues opened/assigned, schedule, explicit prompt).
quote: intelligent mode detection ... @claude mentions in comments ... Issue assignments ... Explicit automation prompts via workflow configuration

claim: Claude Code GitHub Actions ships a flow where @claude mentioned in an issue triggers Claude to read the issue, write the implementation, and open a PR — the action expects contents/issues/pull-requests read+write and recommends a custom GitHub App token over the bundled one when CI must run on Claude's commits.
quote: When someone mentions @claude in an issue, the action triggers Claude to read the issue, understand the request, write the implementation, and open a pull request ... Ensure you're using the GitHub App or custom app (not Actions user), check workflow triggers include the necessary events, and verify app permissions include CI triggers.

claim: IssueOps treats issues as a finite-state machine — labels, comments, and state changes drive automation including branch and PR creation — and keeps the audit trail inside GitHub.
quote: the practice of using GitHub Issues, GitHub Actions, and pull requests (PR) as an interface for automating workflows ... every action leaves a record

claim: Probot is a Node/TypeScript framework for building GitHub Apps that listen to webhook events and perform repository actions including branch and PR operations.
quote: A framework for building GitHub Apps to automate and improve your workflow

claim: create-issue-branch (robvanderleek) is a Probot-based app/Action that creates a branch on issue assignment (auto mode) or when a developer comments /cib (chatops mode), and can auto-open a PR when a label is added.
quote: automates the creation of issue branches, either automatically after assigning an issue or after commenting on an issue with a ChatOps command: /create-issue-branch or /cib

claim: Renovate is the canonical example of a long-running bot that scans repositories on a cadence and opens PRs against multiple platforms (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Azure DevOps, AWS CodeCommit) — running outside of Actions, on its own schedule.
quote: Universal dependency update tool that fits into your workflows ... supports not only a lot of package managers but also multiple platforms like GitHub, GitLab, BitBucket as well as AWS CodeCommit or Azure DevOps

claim: pull_request_target combined with checkout of untrusted PR code is the canonical pwn-request pattern — automation that creates branches/PRs on behalf of external triggers must never do this with a write-scoped token.
quote: Combining pull_request_target workflow trigger with an explicit checkout of an untrusted PR is a dangerous practice that may lead to repository compromise.

claim: peter-evans/repository-dispatch is the de-facto wrapper used inside Actions to fire a repository_dispatch event into another repo, including a JSON client-payload — the typical glue between an external event source and the workflow that opens a PR.
quote: A GitHub action to create a repository dispatch event

claim: actions/create-github-app-token mints an installation token from a GitHub App's private key inside a workflow — the recommended replacement for PATs when you need a token that can trigger downstream workflows.
quote: This app will be used with the actions/create-github-app-token action to generate authentication tokens in your workflows.

claim: gh pr create is the CLI path to opening a PR from a script; --fill auto-derives title/body from commits, and --base/--head let it run non-interactively in CI.
quote: gh pr create --base main --head feature-branch --title "Add feature X" --body "This PR adds feature X which includes..."

claim: Kilo for Slack (Apr 2026) ships a Slack thread → cloud agent → GitHub PR pipeline that goes through the team's existing review workflow — a productized version of the remote-trigger → branch+PR pattern.
quote: the Kilo Slackbot opens a pull request from a Slack thread, it follows the same guardrails teams already rely on today, with the PR going through existing review workflows and approval processes before anything reaches production

claim: GitHub's Copilot coding agent ships as an asynchronous background agent that opens a draft PR, makes changes, then requests review — and (Oct 2025) supports self-hosted runners via Actions Runner Controller for private-infra access.
quote: an asynchronous, autonomous background agent that delegates tasks by opening a draft pull request, making changes in the background, and then requesting a review

claim: octokit-plugin-create-pull-request lets a Node service create a PR with multiple file changes via the GitHub Tree/Commit API — useful when the trigger handler runs outside Actions (your own webhook receiver, Probot app, Lambda).
quote: Octokit plugin to create a pull request with multiple file changes
